stories.headline: Supreme Court Thwarts Trump Bid to Oust Fed’s Cook for Now
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court ruled that Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook can stay in her job for now, reinforcing the central bank’s independence from the White House and dealing a setback to President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve governor Lisa Cook
stories.summary: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.

stories.headline: Sudan says China has waived $50m loan: What’s in it for Khartoum, Beijing?
stories.summary: China and Sudan signed off on a waiver of $50m as Sudan’s military-led government seeks support amid Western sanctions.

stories.headline: Supreme Court blocks Trump from sacking Fed governor Lisa Cook
stories.summary: Justices fail to over-rule the president from firing a regulator in a separate decision

stories.headline: US says it has agreed deal with Iran to halt strikes and resume talks
stories.summary: White House announces Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will fly to Doha for ‘high-level meetings’ but Tehran disputes that further negotiations have been confirmed

stories.headline: Supreme Court rejects Trump's appeal of E Jean Carroll's sexual abuse case
stories.summary: The decision means the US president will now have to pay her the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Rules Lisa Cook Can Remain at Fed for Now
stories.summary: The US Supreme Court refused to let President Donald Trump immediately fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook. In a 5-4 vote, the high court said Cook can stay in her job while she fights Trump’s bid to oust her over unproven mortgage fraud allegations. Michael McKee reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Supreme Court, for now, blocks Trump from firing Federal Reserve Board appointee
stories.summary: The decision is a setback for Trump, who alleged that Lisa Cook committed mortgage fraud and has complained that the Fed is not dropping interest rates quickly enough.

stories.headline: Supreme Court lets stand $5 million civil verdict against Trump in Carroll case
stories.summary: A 2023 trial found President Donald Trump liable for allegedly sexually assaulting journalist E. Jean Carroll. The decision Monday leaves in place a ruling affirming the judgment.

stories.headline: Supreme Court Rejects Trump’s Request to Appeal $5 Million Verdict in E. Jean Carroll Case
stories.summary: President Trump had asked the justices to intervene after a jury found that he had sexually abused and defamed the writer E. Jean Carroll.

stories.headline: Russian missile and drone attacks kill at least eight in Ukraine
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy condemns the 'horrific attacks' and urges allies to boost Ukraine's air defences.

stories.headline: ‘A concession to Zelenskyy’s ultimatum’: Ukraine’s triumph over Belarus
stories.summary: President Zelenskyy demands Lukashenko shut down stations installed by Russia 'or we will'.

stories.headline: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
stories.summary: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

stories.headline: Three Firefighters Die as Wildfires Ravage Utah and Colorado
stories.summary: Their bodies were taken by helicopter to a Colorado airport, where soot-covered firefighters drove them to the coroner’s office. Fires are raging in the arid Southwest after a warm winter.

stories.summary: Sudan's el-Obeid faces mass atrocities, like el-Fasher in 2025, the UN warns.

stories.headline: DR Congo bans mass gatherings in the  capital to prevent spread of Ebola
stories.summary: Opposition politicians accuse the government of using the outbreak to halt a planned protest.

stories.headline: Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan
stories.summary: The Taliban government says civilians were killed, while Pakistan maintains they targeted militants.

stories.headline: Teyana Taylor's tears and Lauryn Hill tribute steal show at BET Awards
stories.summary: Taylor had an emotional reaction to being presented with the Icon of the Year award by Janet Jackson.

stories.summary: Deputy crime intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was due to appear before an inquiry into police corruption this week.

stories.headline: Kenya, Congo Eurobonds Among Top Winners From Iran War Trade Unwind
stories.summary: Kenya and Democratic Republic of Congo are among the top performing African debt markets this month, as tumbling crude prices lure investors back to securities issued by oil importers.

stories.headline: Microsoft’s $530 Billion Rout Sets Up Its Worst Month Since 2008
stories.summary: Microsoft Corp. shares are heading for their worst month in years as investors continue to fret about how the software giant will fare in a world marked by artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Why Pakistan’s Afghan air strikes aren’t stopping armed attacks
stories.summary: Analysts warn military pressure alone cannot contain a threat from armed groups that has reached Pakistan's cities.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquake rescues: Five heart-warming moments
stories.summary: Amid the death and destruction, a few fleeting moments have brought rare relief to Venezuelans.

stories.headline: Saudi Arabia’s early FIFA World Cup exit prompts federation chief to resign
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia, the three-time Asian champions, finish bottom of Group H with two points from three games.

stories.headline: Australia and Vanuatu sign deal to block foreign military bases
stories.summary: Two countries sign economic and security pact, as China expresses concern that the agreement may be targeted at it.

stories.headline: Vanuatu, Australia ink deal to bar foreign military bases in Pacific nation
Cyprus’ tourism revenues fell by more than 35% in April as a result of the war
stories.summary: Despite an initially expected downturn, Deputy Tourism Minister Kostas Koumis said tourism in Cyprus had returned to a "stable course", adding that improving performance in recent months showed government measures had "a positive impact".

stories.headline: Government extends anti-crisis plan and starts work on 2027 budget
stories.summary: In an extraordinary meeting, the Council of Ministers extends economic measures against the global crisis, unveils a housing plan and updates the macroeconomic framework for drafting the state budget.

stories.headline: The AI boom propping up markets could trigger the next crash, central banks warn
stories.summary: The vast surge of investment in AI, which has powered global stock markets to record highs, risks ending in a financial bust, the Bank for International Settlements warns, as the build-up’s hidden costs begin to surface in company accounts and consumer prices alike.

stories.headline: South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss
stories.summary: South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.

stories.headline: Biden blasts Trump 'vanity projects', calling him a 'loser' at fundraising event

stories.headline: South Korea unveils $880bn chip and AI investment plan
stories.summary: It comes as regional rivals like Taiwan, China and Japan are investing heavily in chip factories and other technology.

stories.headline: China grounds light aircraft after Beijing tower crash

stories.headline: World Cup to Drive Billions in Betting Volume
stories.summary:
This year’s World Cup is the first since prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket exploded to popularity as a new way to bet on sports.
Chad Beynon, Senior Gaming, Lodging, and Theaters Analyst at Macquarie, talks about how the World Cup is impacting the US sports betting market and which stocks stand to benefit. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Comcast Rises on Split; Iridium Up on Rocket Lab Deal | Stock Movers
stories.summary:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Comcast (CMCSA) is rising after it announced its intention to separate into two independent publicly traded companies through a tax-free spin-off of NBCUniversal and Sky. Upon completion of the transaction, Comcast shareholders will own shares in both Comcast and NBCUniversal.

stories.headline: Yen Hits Four-Decade Low in Historic Slide That’s Rattled Japan
stories.summary: The yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986, a milestone that will generate unease in Japan and put traders on high alert for authorities wading into the market.

stories.summary: The aircraft belonged to state oil giant Aramco and the cause of the crash is being investigated.

stories.headline: Oil Trades Near Four-Month Low as US and Iran Halt Fresh Attacks
stories.summary: Oil held above the lowest since the end of February after the US and Iran agreed to stop attacking each other, following flare-ups over the weekend that saw a supertanker hit near the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Tech Giants Power Stock Gains After AI-Fueled Rout: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A rally in several technology giants lifted stocks after last week’s artificial-intelligence selloff lured buyers betting the industry’s investment boom will continue to support solid corporate earnings.

stories.headline: Colorado’s Primary for Governor Pivots to Trump and Who Will Fight Him
stories.summary: The Democratic primary on Tuesday is likely to decide the next governor, and Senator Michael Bennet, once seen as a shoo-in, is in a dogfight with Attorney General Phil Weiser.

stories.headline: Israel strikes southern Lebanon as Hezbollah condemns new deal
stories.summary: Strikes have killed at least one person, state media say a day after Lebanon and Israel signed a framework agreement.

stories.summary: In La Guaira, desperate families keep vigil at buildings where they fear their relatives are trapped, but face an impossible task to move heavy debris.

stories.headline: Why Kim Jong Un never talks about his mother - or her controversial bloodline

stories.summary: Supporters of incumbent Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan say a challenger with the same name is not running in “good faith,” but a judge says that is not an issue.

stories.headline: Christmas market attacker jailed for life for murdering six in Germany
stories.summary: A nine-year-old and five women were killed when Taleb Al-Abdulmohsen drove into the market in 2024.

stories.summary: Hezbollah is not party to Friday's agreement, and previous ceasefires between Israel and Lebanon have still seen near-daily cross-border strikes.

stories.headline: Rainbow flags await Egypt and Iran at awkward Pride Match

stories.summary: Ukraine's Volodmyr Zelensky skips major conference aimed at rebuilding his country after being stripped of a Polish state honour.

stories.summary: Traffic in the waterway has risen since the US and Iran signed a deal aimed at ending the war, including 42 ships on Saturday alone.

stories.headline: Israeli troops kill two in south Lebanon after lull in fighting, authorities say

stories.headline: UN commission of inquiry says Israel committing genocide in Gaza by deliberately targeting children

stories.headline: Leading Pakistan activist given life sentence over soldier's killing at rally

stories.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
